codeReview.summary: The scheduler claim path, bounded backoff, and concurrency tests are solid, but the feature is not safe around MR cancellation: retry-scheduled runs are not cancelled on close/merge, and in-flight cancellations can be overwritten back to completed/failed after the processor returns.

issues.description: Close/merge cancellation only updates rows currently in `pending` or `running`, but the scheduler treats `status='failed'` rows with a due `next_retry_at` as claimable work (`internal/db/queries/review_runs.sql:13-16,70-73`). After this feature adds retry scheduling, a run that temporarily fails and is waiting for retry survives MR close/merge and can still be claimed and processed on a closed or merged MR.

After `ProcessRun` returns, the scheduler unconditionally marks the row `completed` or `failed` (`internal/scheduler/service.go:269-317`; `internal/db/queries/review_runs.sql:42-68`) without checking whether another path has already cancelled the run. If a close/merge event cancels a running run via `CancelPendingRunsForMR` while work is in flight (`internal/db/queries/review_runs.sql:70-73`), the worker can overwrite `cancelled` and allow downstream work to finish anyway.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared guidance does not explicitly call out that retry-scheduled runs are represented as `status='failed'` plus `next_retry_at`, i.e. still-active work that lifecycle code must treat like pending work for cancellation and orchestration decisions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md` requires retryable failures with backoff (VAL-INGRESS-014); `internal/db/queries/review_runs.sql:13-16` makes those rows claimable, but `internal/db/queries/review_runs.sql:70-73` / `internal/runs/service.go:219` only cancel `pending` and `running` rows.

summary: Fail. The feature correctly adds atomic claiming and retry scheduling, but two blocking cancellation gaps remain: retry-scheduled failed runs are not cancelled on MR close/merge, and an in-flight cancelled run can be overwritten back to completed/failed by the scheduler. Review report written for commit 3682c71.
